claim: CEProcessFill and CEProcessRecoveredPending each produce an MFR+Snapshot pair atomically, consuming 2 sequence numbers per fill. The Snapshot carries no payload distinction from MFR in the spec (both are type-tagged records in ce_log with identical structure [type, seq, session]). No invariant or downstream action distinguishes MFR from Snapshot in its guards or cursor logic (OMSConsumeCE advances cursor by eff.seq = cursor+1 regardless of type, CEBackupApply applies by seq regardless of type, ArchiverIngestCE stores regardless of type). Collapsing MFR+Snapshot into a single MFR effect per fill halves the sequence-space cost of fills, reduces ce_log growth rate for fill-heavy workloads, and simplifies CEProcessFill and CEProcessRecoveredPending from 2-effect atomic production to 1-effect production, eliminating the ce_seq+1<=MaxSeq guard strengthening (only ce_seq<=MaxSeq needed).

properties_checked.P2_CESequenceMonotonic: SAFE. Each fill still produces exactly one uniquely-sequenced effect. Uniqueness within session preserved.
properties_checked.P4_SurgicalRollbackPrecision: SAFE. ce_last_recovered_seq is computed from archiver contents (MaxContiguousFrom). With fewer effects per fill, archiver stores are smaller but contiguity is preserved identically. Old-session bounding unchanged.
properties_checked.P9_CELogBounded: SAFE. Each produced effect has seq = ce_seq (before increment). ce_seq' = ce_seq+1. e.seq < ce_seq' holds.
properties_checked.P12_CEBackupAppliedValid: SAFE. Backup applies effects by seq from msgs_ce_broadcast. With one effect per fill instead of two, backup applies one seq per fill. Each applied seq still has a corresponding ce_log entry.
properties_checked.P1_TypeInvariant: SAFE. ce_log subset of [type: CEEffectTypes, seq: 1..MaxSeq, session: Nat]. MFR is in CEEffectTypes. Snapshot simply stops being produced; no type violation.
properties_checked.P6_PhantomLeaderRejection: UNAFFECTED. About authority_ts, not effect production.
properties_checked.P16_CEPendingRecoveryBounded: UNAFFECTED. About ob_archiver_pending bounds, not CE effect count.
properties_checked.P17_DownstreamCacheCoherence: UNAFFECTED. About cached timestamps, not effect production.
properties_checked.P18_OMSMutedUntilCaughtUp: SAFE. ce_last_recovered_seq is independent of per-fill seq consumption (computed from archiver contiguity, not ce_seq). OMS cursor advances 1 per CE effect consumed. With fewer effects total, OMS catches up faster (liveness improvement). Safety condition (cursor >= last_recovered) preserved because both sides scale proportionally.
properties_checked.P19_CEArchiverSafetyGuarantee: SAFE. Three-way disjunction checked per s in ce_backup_applied. Each fill produces 1 backup-applicable seq instead of 2. Each such seq has corresponding broadcast message and ce_log entry. All three legs of disjunction hold for the single seq.

risk_notes: Snapshot type remains in CEEffectTypes but is never produced. This is harmless (empty subset). Could be removed from CEEffectTypes as a follow-up cosmetic change, but that touches the CONSTANTS section and is out of scope.

OMS log entries from CE will no longer include Snapshot-typed effects. OMS INV8 (OMSDerivedState) checks e.type in CEEffectTypes union OBEffectTypes union {PendingFill}. MFR is in CEEffectTypes, so this holds.
Downstream consumers that expect alternating MFR/Snapshot pairs at the implementation level would need adjustment. In the formal spec, no such expectation exists (all consumption is type-agnostic).
The ce_seq space is consumed at half the rate for fill-heavy workloads, extending MaxSeq headroom. This is a pure benefit with no safety implication.

diff.description: Collapse MFR+Snapshot pair into single MFR effect in CEProcessFill and CEProcessRecoveredPending

before: LET mfr == [type |-> "MFR", seq |-> ce_seq, session |-> ce_primary]
{[type |-> "MFR", seq |-> ce_seq, session |-> ce_primary, market |-> msg.market]}
after: LET mfr == [type |-> "MFR", seq |-> ce_seq, session |-> ce_primary]
{[type |-> "MFR", seq |-> ce_seq, session |-> ce_primary, market |-> msg.market]}

before: /\ LET mfr == [type |-> "MFR", seq |-> ce_seq, session |-> ce_primary]
after: /\ LET mfr == [type |-> "MFR", seq |-> ce_seq, session |-> ce_primary]

structural_delta.guard_simplifications: ce_seq+1<=MaxSeq weakened to ce_seq<=MaxSeq (same as CEProduceDirectEffect)

lessons_respected: LESSON 1: Does not touch ce_checkpoint_seq or its cross-session persistence
LESSON 2: Does not touch ce_backup_applied contiguity or ArchiverGCByAcks
LESSON 3: Does not modify CEViewChange or CEBackupApply
